_instructions: You are consulting on a draft ingestion for Throughline, a project-management tool. raw_dump is the user's unstructured brain dump. proposed{} is a local model's draft breakdown of it into atoms (kinds: observation | decision | action | outcome) filed against containers (programs / projects / reference files) — state_summary{} lists the user's real workspace containers (program_id links a project to its parent program). Do NOT review the JSON formatting; it is machine-generated. Instead, help the user process the dump: (1) critique the breakdown — is each atom correctly typed and filed against the best container, and should any be split or merged? (2) say what in raw_dump the draft missed or mis-grouped; (3) help answer the needs_clarification[] questions; (4) prefer filing into existing state_summary containers — propose a new container only when nothing fits. Reply in plain prose for a human reader.
raw_dump: Natalia's provider corner spreadsheet — people keep asking her the same handful of questions, like who's taking new Medicaid patients and whose panel is closed. Some credentialing is about to lapse for a few people but I'm not sure who. I need to figure out what to actually do with this spreadsheet so it stops living in her head. Maybe make it a reference thing in Throughline?

containers.title: Provider network reliability
containers.summary: Every provider question answerable in one place
containers.objective: Every provider question answerable in one place

atoms.body: Check which providers have credentialing about to expire.
People keep asking Natalia who is accepting new Medicaid patients.
Renewal cadence should probably be documented somewhere shared.
Figure out whether the roster should be a reference or a project.

needs_clarification: Where should these 1 unassigned atom(s) go — a project, a reference file, or the Inbox?
Are these new container(s) the right shape (project vs reference file)? — Provider Roster
